header.nav.planner_desc: Inventory-based metallurgy planning and batch targets.

header.nav.leather_desc: Hide tanning, pelt curing, and barrel planning.
crucible.title: Crucible Inputs
crucible.description: Add metals to your crucible. Each slot can hold 0-128 nuggets of a single metal type.
crucible.slot.label: Slot {n}
composition.title: Current Composition
result.title: Alloy Result
result.empty: Add metals to the crucible to see alloy results
result.no_match: No known alloy from this composition
result.valid_message: This composition creates a valid {alloy} alloy!
result.not_enough: Not enough material: You need {units} more units ({nuggets} nuggets) to make 1 ingot (100 units minimum).
result.contamination: Contamination detected: This alloy contains metals that should not be present: {metals}
planner.title: Metallurgy Planner
planner.description: Plan from your inventory, turn alloys into real crucible runs, and compare scarcity tradeoffs before spending rare metals.
planner.discovery.label: Discovery mode
planner.mode.label: Scarcity mode
planner.inventory.title: Inventory-driven planning
planner.inventory.description: Enter your current nuggets by metal. The planner searches only what you can actually execute with real crucible constraints.
planner.target.title: Target alloy plan
planner.target.description: Pick a recipe, set a target, and the planner will build executable runs that stay within four crucible slots.
planner.target.minimum_valid: Smallest valid batch from this stock: {n}
planner.target.previous_valid: Closest valid target at or below your request: {n}
planner.target.next_valid: Next valid breakpoint above your request: {n}
planner.target.next_valid_none: No higher valid breakpoint from current stock
planner.target.invalid_target: {n} ingots is not a valid breakpoint for this inventory. Closest valid at or below: {previous}. Next valid breakpoint: {next}.
planner.run.title: Run {n}
planner.run.subtitle: {ingots} ingots from {consumed}
planner.find.empty: Add inventory to see which alloys are currently craftable.
leather.title: Leatherworking Planner
leather.description: Plan both tanning and pelt curing with game-accurate barrels, tannin, fat, water, and solvent costs.
leather.summary.title: Leather Summary
leather.summary.description: Actual output after rounding to real hide counts and tannin batches.
leather.summary.pelt_description: Quick-read curing output, fat cost, and optional bear split results.
leather.summary.ready_first_leather: Pull the shopping list before you touch barrels. Water, logs, and solvent are the real prep cost.
leather.summary.ready_first_pelt: Pelt curing is front-loaded: get the fat and hides together, then the rest is mostly waiting.
leather.summary.bottleneck_leather: The long wait is still the tannin chain. Hide barrels are easy to read; tannin production is where most players under-plan.
leather.summary.bottleneck_bear: Bear tanning starts with raw large or huge hides, but the tannin stages scale off the huge hides you get after scraping.
leather.summary.bottleneck_pelt: There is no barrel bottleneck here. The only real constraint is fat on day one and the full 48-hour cure.
leather.summary.bottleneck_bear_pelt: Bear pelts cure simply, but the split result matters because the head and body serve different recipes.
leather.shopping.title: Shopping List
leather.shopping.description: Gather these materials before starting the workflow.
leather.pipeline.time_note: All durations are in-game time, not real time.
leather.pipeline.title: Workflow
leather.pipeline.description_leather: Read this as a production track: tannin and soaking can overlap, then the hide states progress in order.
leather.pipeline.description_pelt: Pelts are shorter to read: oil, wait, then optionally split bear pelts for heads and generic hides.
leather.notes.solvent: Wiki-verified mechanic: lime matches liters directly, while powdered borax is gathered in 5L batches of diluted borax.
leather.notes.mixing: Leatherworking only handles one hide size family per run. If you want to process multiple sizes, split them into separate calculations.
leather.notes.pelt_mode: Pelt recipes are easier to reason about from the hides you already have, so this workflow stays in “I have hides” mode.
leather.notes.small_variants: Fox, arctic fox, and raccoon all use the small-hide path.
leather.notes.bear: Bear hides change size after scraping, so the soaking and tannin stages must be planned separately.
leather.notes.bear_supported_leather: Bear tanning is now supported with species-specific raw soak size and scraped huge-hide output based on the Vintage Story wiki.
leather.notes.bear_supported_pelt: Bear pelt curing is separate from leatherworking: cure the oiled hide for 48 hours, then split the head and body if you need generic pelts.
leather.notes.bear_pelt: Bear pelts cure with the head attached. Split them afterward if you need a head or generic pelts.
leather.notes.pelt_simple: Generic pelts are a short two-step process: oil the hide, then let it cure for 48 hours.
leather.notes.pelt_storage: Curing keeps progressing wherever the oiled hides are stored. Temperature and container choice do not speed it up.
reference.title: Alloy Reference
reference.shared.title: Shared Reference
reference.shared.description: Switch between metallurgy data and leatherworking mechanics without leaving the reference surface.
reference.leather.title: Leatherwork Reference
reference.leather.description: Core hide yields, solvent mechanics, tannin batching, and bear-specific workflow notes gathered into one browseable reference.
reference.leather.tanning.title: Tanning chain
reference.leather.tanning.body: Leather runs follow the same sequence every time: soak hides, scrape them, prepare them in weak tannin, convert the reserve into strong tannin, then finish the leather.
reference.leather.pelts.title: Pelt curing
reference.leather.pelts.body: Pelts are a separate path. Oil the hide with fat, wait 48 in-game hours, then optionally split bear pelts into heads and body pelts.
reference.leather.solvent.title: Solvents
reference.leather.solvent.body: Lime matches soaking liters directly. Powdered borax is gathered by preparing diluted borax in {liters}L batches at {cost} powder per batch.
reference.leather.hide_profiles.title: Hide sizes
reference.leather.hide_profiles.subtitle: Raw hide throughput
reference.leather.hide_profiles.liters: {liters} L soaking liquid per raw hide
reference.leather.small_variants.title: Small-animal mapping
reference.leather.small_variants.body: Fox, arctic fox, and raccoon all use the standard small-hide rules for tanning and pelt curing.
reference.leather.tannin_chain.title: Tannin chain
reference.leather.tannin_chain.weak_body: Each weak-tannin batch produces {liters} L. Leather plans reserve one full weak-tannin stage for preparing hides and another for the later strong-tannin conversion.
reference.leather.tannin_chain.strong_bo: Strong tannin is made by combining the reserved weak tannin with another log batch. It is the last liquid stage before hides become leather.
reference.leather.workflow_timing.title: Workflow timing
reference.leather.workflow_timing.soak_b: Soaking takes 20 in-game hours and uses the raw-hide size, even for bear hides.
reference.leather.workflow_timing.prepar: Prepared hides sit in weak tannin for 3 in-game days. This is where scraped hide size matters.
reference.leather.workflow_timing.finish: The final strong-tannin step takes 4.5 in-game days. Tannin production can overlap with earlier hide stages.
reference.leather.bears.title: Bear support
reference.leather.pelt_reference.title: Pelt rules
reference.leather.pelt_reference.generic: Small hides oil in groups of four, medium hides in pairs, large hides singly, and huge hides in pairs of fat. Every oiled hide then cures for 48 in-game hours.
reference.leather.pelt_reference.bear_bo: Bear pelts cure with the head attached. After curing, you can split them to get a bear head plus the species-specific count of generic pelts from the body.
overview.hero.title: Metallurgy, leatherwork, and reference in one place
overview.hero.description: Use the alloy calculator and planner when you are working the crucible, switch to leatherwork for hides and pelts, and keep the shared reference nearby for the exact game rules that drive both.
overview.metallurgy.title: Metallurgy tools
overview.metallurgy.body: Work from exact crucible composition or from inventory. Calculator and planner stay deep-linkable under the metallurgy path.
overview.leather.title: Leatherwork planner
overview.leather.body: Plan tanning, pelt curing, barrel load, tannin, solvent, water, and bear-hide edge cases from one leather workflow.
overview.reference.title: Shared reference
overview.reference.body: Keep alloy ranges, hide outputs, solvents, tannin rules, and bear notes in one cross-domain reference surface instead of scattering them by tool.
overview.workflow.title: How the app is split
overview.workflow.metallurgy_body: Calculator and planner remain tool-first pages: you land there when you need an answer for the crucible right now.
overview.workflow.leather_body: Leather planning is organized around hide state changes, barrel timing, and shopping-list prep rather than a single formula screen.
overview.faq.title: Quick FAQ
overview.faq.q1.question: Why move overview and reference out of metallurgy?
overview.faq.q1.answer: Because the app now covers two top-level domains. Shared pages make more sense as app-level surfaces instead of pretending they belong only to metallurgy.
overview.faq.q2.question: Do metallurgy links still work?
overview.faq.q2.answer: Yes. Calculator and planner stay under /metallurgy/, and legacy support-page URLs can still resolve to the new shared pages.
overview.faq.q3.question: What is shared now?
overview.faq.q3.answer: The landing page, the reference surface, locale switching, SEO handling, and shell navigation are now shared across metallurgy and leatherwork.

privacy.title: Privacy
privacy.analytics.title: Analytics
privacy.analytics.body_after: , a privacy-friendly analytics tool. It collects anonymous page views only: no cookies, no personal data, no IP addresses, and no cross-site tracking.
privacy.storage.title: Local Storage
privacy.storage.body_before: This site does not use cookies for tracking or advertising. The only browser storage used is
privacy.storage.body_after: to remember your selected language and theme.
privacy.compliance.title: Compliance
privacy.compliance.body: Because no personal data is collected, this site can operate without a cookie consent banner under GDPR, CCPA, and the ePrivacy Directive.
